questions.question: Why should a search input ref be initialized with '' instead of null?
options: null causes a runtime error when bound with v-model
v-model on inputs always produces strings, so '' matches the output type and avoids null checks
Vue's template compiler rejects null as a ref initial value

questions.question: When IS null the appropriate initial value for a ref?
options: Always: it is the Vue recommended default
When 'no value' is semantically different from 'empty', such as data not yet loaded
When you want to skip TypeScript type checking

questions.question: What TypeScript problem arises from initializing ref<string | null>(null) used with a text input?
options: The template will not compile without explicit typing
String methods like .toLowerCase() cause TS errors because the value is possibly null
TypeScript infers the type as unknown instead of string
v-model refuses to bind to a nullable ref
